mnemonics: WRITE a COPY later - Imagine a parent and child process sharing a book. They only get their own COPY to WRITE in if they need to change the story, otherwise they just share the original.
keyPoints: A memory management technique where parent and child processes initially share the same pages in memory.
A private copy of a page is only created for a process when it attempts to write to that shared page.
It is an optimization that saves memory and time by deferring copying until absolutely necessary.
commonMistakes: Confusing it with simply sharing memory; the key differentiator is the deferred copy-on-write action.
Assuming the copy is made immediately upon process creation instead of only on the first write attempt.
